prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `67c52801`.
codex_response:
Implemented `67c52801` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: keep the solid bottom floor and the penultimate-row floor segments, then move each upper rectangle into one zero-gap on the penultimate row so its width matches that gap, rotating when the matching dimension is the rectangle’s height.

`arc2_opus46_summary.json` was a useful starting hint. `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` was discarded and corrected in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_67c52801/notes.md) because it misread the penultimate row as markers instead of floor gaps. The generator matches the task family by building sparse upper rectangles, a gapped floor row, and only emitting cases with a unique valid gap-to-rectangle assignment. No new primitive was needed.

Validation passed: official examples `5/5`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked a generated preview sheet.
